title: Adhesive Bonding Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend bonding machines that use adhesives to join items for further processing or to form a completed product. Processes include joining veneer sheets into plywood; gluing paper; or joining rubber and rubberized fabric parts, plastic, simulated leather, or other materials.

tasks.text: Align and position materials being joined to ensure accurate application of adhesive or heat sealing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust machine components according to specifications such as widths, lengths, and thickness of materials and amounts of glue, cement, or adhesive required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and report or resolve problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Start machines, and turn valves or move controls to feed, admit, apply, or transfer materials and adhesives, and to adjust temperature, pressure, and time settings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fill machines with glue, cement, or adhesives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform test production runs and make adjustments as necessary to ensure that completed products meet standards and specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices such as tape measures, gauges, or calipers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read work orders and communicate with coworkers to determine machine and equipment settings and adjustments and supply and product specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove and stack completed materials or products, and restock materials to be joined.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe gauges, meters, and control panels to obtain information about equipment temperatures and pressures, or the speed of feeders or conveyors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain production records such as quantities, dimensions, and thicknesses of materials processed.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Remove jammed materials from machines and readjust components as necessary to resume normal operations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mount or load material such as paper, plastic, wood, or rubber in feeding mechanisms of cementing or gluing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Transport materials, supplies, and finished products between storage and work areas, using forklifts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and maintain gluing and cementing machines, using solutions, lubricants, brushes, and scrapers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure and mix ingredients to prepare glue.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
